summary: Iter86 — Challenge d'ami (Duel) backend + frontend smoke. 11/15 backend tests PASS, 1 FAIL (cascade from skipped opponent), 3 SKIPPED (opponent-side tests — OTP-based registration blocked creating a 2nd opponent account in headless). Admin config extension (cfg-duel-enabled, cfg-quiz-timer, cfg-duel-winner-bonus, cfg-duel-loser-bonus, cfg-duel-accepts-per-day) verified present on /admin → Roue Fortune → Configuration tab. Quiz timer-locked-at-start behaviour verified end-to-end: admin set=15s, mid-session change to 10s does NOT retroactively invalidate the in-flight run. Duel enabled toggle correctly returns 503 'Challenge d'ami temporairement désactivé.' when OFF. create-from-tap contract (share_token 22 chars + share_url /duel/{token} + share_card_url + expires_at ≈24h) validated. Public /api/duel/{token} preview returns challenger name + avatar + score. Self-duel blocked (HTTP 400). /api/duel/my/list returns duels. /api/duel/admin/overview (admin only) shape validated: total/open/in_progress/completed/expired/conversion_rate/timeseries/top_challengers. Bob auth-denied on admin overview (correct 401/403).

minor.issue: Opponent full flow (accept + submit with score 80 vs 65, winner bonus=50) could not be executed end-to-end in automated suite because creating a 2nd opponent account via /api/auth/register requires OTP activation. Main agent smoke-tested this manually (Bob 65 → Admin 80 → Admin wins bonus 50). Keeping it as manual-verified. Test cascade: test_opponent_accepts_and_submits SKIPPED → test_completed_duel_status FAILED (duel still 'open').

action_items: Main agent: manually (or via seeded 2nd test user with active=true) verify the opponent-side tap duel winner/loser bonus award — already smoke-tested per handoff. Consider adding a pre-seeded active 2nd test user (e.g. alice@japap.com / Test1234!) to /app/memory/test_credentials.md to enable full opponent-side automation next iteration.
Optionally: expose daily-cap and expired-duel scenarios via an admin debug endpoint (e.g. POST /api/duel/admin/{token}/expire) to make those paths deterministically testable.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/duel.py is 782 lines — borderline acceptable, but consider splitting admin/overview + start/submit per game into separate modules if it grows further.
routes/duel.py L312: expires_at compared to naive vs tz-aware datetime — code uses datetime.now(timezone.utc) and DB column is TIMESTAMPTZ, so OK.

routes/duel.py L440: opponent_id != user on submit-quiz correctly returns 403; but test_second_submit_rejected passes run_id=1 which is a different user's run — backend returns 403 'pas l'adversaire' first, which the test's message check tolerates. Logic is sound.
routes/quiz.py L255: effective_limit read from run['time_limit_s'] — locked-at-start behaviour confirmed working.
routes/duel.py L107-110: duel_enabled gate is applied to create + opponent start/submit. Good.
routes/duel.py L319-331: daily accept cap read from admin config, applied before opponent_id is set. Correct — cap only triggers on NEW acceptance, not re-entry.

routes/duel.py L515-524: winner/loser bonuses routed through points_service add_points with source='wheel'. This means duel bonuses compete for wheel cycle budget rather than quiz/tap budgets — design choice, documented via metadata['kind']. Worth double-checking this is intentional (vs source='quiz' / 'tap' matching the game).
Admin controls (cfg-duel-*) render inside the Roue Fortune → Configuration sub-tab as expected and all 5 testids render cleanly.

success_rate.backend: 73% (11/15 — 3 skipped due to OTP-gated 2nd user, 1 cascade fail)

test_credentials: admin@japap.com / JapapAdmin2024! ; bob@japap.com / Test1234! ; TEST_opp_iter86@japap.com requires OTP activation (not usable for automation)
seed_data_creation: Created 1 tap duel from Bob (challenger_score=65). Used /api/tap/admin/reset-user/{uid} + /api/quiz/admin/reset-user/{uid} between runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://japap-refactor.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iter86_duel.py -v -p no:ethereum (~115s, 15 tests). To unlock opponent-side automation, add a 2nd pre-activated test account (e.g. alice@japap.com / Test1234!) to the DB seed. Without that, 3 tests will skip and 1 will cascade-fail. Duels: DELETE FROM duels WHERE challenger_id='<bob_uid>' OR opponent_id='<bob_uid>' between runs if you want a fresh slate. Admin UI path: /admin → 'Roue Fortune' tab → 'Configuration' sub-tab → cfg-duel-* controls present.
rca of the issue: 4 non-passing tests are all downstream of a single blocker: automated creation of a second opponent user account. /api/auth/register returns 200 but the account stays inactive until email OTP verification. Tests then skip on opp_sess fixture. Only one test (test_completed_duel_status) fails rather than skips because it doesn't depend on opp_sess directly — it reads the public duel after the opponent flow should have completed. Fix is to seed an already-activated 2nd user in test fixtures, which requires backend admin seed support or directly setting users.active=TRUE in test setup.
